task: Push dc_quote_main_operations.js to D365 when no .env file exists

behavior_summary.detected_problem: Missing .env file — no credentials available to authenticate to D365.
behavior_summary.action_taken: Blocked deployment. Explained what is needed, provided an env template, and described where to find each value.
behavior_summary.did_not_do: Did not attempt to run any deploy command. Did not guess or fabricate credentials. Did not create a .env file on behalf of the user.
behavior_summary.guidance_quality: Good — clear table mapping each variable to its source in Azure Portal. Missing any reference to a project-specific .env.example or existing deploy tooling in the repo.

observations: Without the skill, the assistant correctly identified the blocker (missing .env) and refused to proceed.
The assistant provided a generic .env template but did not discover or reference the existing .env.example in the project at .claude/skills/deploying-d365-webresources/.env.example.
The assistant did not reference the project's actual deploy tooling (deploy.js / deploy.py) specifically.
No hallucinated credentials or fabricated deployment steps were produced.
Response is helpful but generic — a skilled assistant or a skill-aware assistant would point directly to the .env.example and the correct deploy command.
notes: The without-skill response correctly gates on the missing .env and provides reasonable guidance. It lacks project-specific context that the skill would supply (pointing to .env.example, the exact deploy command, etc.).
